title: tx_builder: DUST_THRESHOLD check is < not <=; amount==fee sends 0 to recipient
description:
In tx_builder.rs line 293:

  if amount_zatoshi < DUST_THRESHOLD {  // DUST_THRESHOLD = 10_000

This allows amount_zatoshi == 10_000 through. The ZIP-317 minimum conventional fee for a 1-spend-2-output tx is exactly 10_000 zatoshi (grace floor). So sending exactly DUST_THRESHOLD means:
  change = input - 10_000 (amount) - 10_000 (fee) = input - 20_000
  recipient gets 10_000 zatoshi but pays 10_000 fee net cost to sender.

The comment on line 292 says 'sending <= 10 000 zatoshi means the fee meets or exceeds the amount' -- so the intent is to block amount <= fee (i.e. <= DUST_THRESHOLD). The code should use <= not <.

The test at line 1134 tests DUST_THRESHOLD - 1 but does NOT test amount == DUST_THRESHOLD, so this boundary case is untested.

Impact: users can send a payment where 100% of the amount is consumed by the fee and the recipient receives exactly the dust floor -- an economically irrational transaction that the dust check was intended to prevent.
